source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.



The Opportunity

The Product Marketing Analyst will play a critical role in scaling Sequoia’s Product Marketing and Market Insights capabilities at a time of increasing demand from Sales and Growth teams. As prospect engagement deepens and Market Insights program expands, this role provides the analytical and operational backbone needed to improve speed, quality, and win rates. You will sit at the intersection of data, insights, and execution. Through analyzing CRM win/loss trends and sales enablement effectiveness, this role ensures the Product Marketing team can operate with greater precision, consistency, and impact. Based in Mexico City, you’ll enable global collaboration while providing focused support on time-intensive, insight-driven work.



What You Get to Do:

 - Analyze CRM opportunity data to identify win/loss drivers, competitive patterns, and persona-level insights.

 - Conduct sales collateral adherence and adoption analysis to assess utilization, effectiveness, and gaps across the field.

 - Support market insights initiatives by analyzing qualitative and quantitative data related to customer needs, positioning, and competitive intelligence.

 - Translate complex data sets into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for Product Marketing, Sales, and cross-functional partners.

 - Improve operational efficiency within Product Marketing by standardizing analysis, reporting, and insight-sharing processes.



What You Bring:

 - 3–5 years of experience in marketing, product marketing, or a related analytical role.

 - Bachelor’s degree in marketing, business, or a related field.

 - Strong analytical mindset with the ability to synthesize data into trends tactical recommendations.

 - Hands-on experience with CRM systems, marketing tools, and data visualization/reporting.

 - High attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

 - Clear, confident communicator who can explain insights to marketing leadership.

 - Self-starter with strong ownership mindset and comfort operating with limited oversight.

 - English Proficiency.



Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building 

Sequoia provides equal opportunity to all applicants without regard to race, color, creed, religion, citizenship, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, marital status, military or veteran status, disability, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

Compensation & Benefits
Sequoia provides competitive compensation including base salary, performance-based bonus programs, and comprehensive benefits package.

Sequoia’s Candidate Privacy Policy 

https://www.sequoia.com/legal/candidate-privacy-policy/

source_jobs.title: Global Advisor MX
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.


What You Get to Do:

The Global Advisor role is responsible for managing clients across multiple countries. Responsibilities include implementing and servicing global and local plans, project management, strategy development and execution, and providing services to clients through the following functions:

 - Oversee all aspects of global client management, including implementing benefit plans, providing daily support, building client relationships, and managing renewals.

 - Offer strategic analysis and consulting expertise for clients’ international benefits programs, helping them design and improve these offerings.

 - Work collaboratively with team members on complex, global projects that require input from multiple experts.

 - Educate clients about benefit plan management and ensure compliance with both country-specific and local regulations.

 - Advise on multinational benefit solutions, such as international pooling, captives, global underwriting, and other financial arrangements.

 - Provide guidance on programs for global travelers, medical coverage, and expatriate employee benefits.

 - Manage projects for global clients to ensure they are completed successfully and on time.

 - Communicate effectively with all levels of a client’s organization, from local HR teams to senior leaders like the CEO and CFO.

 - Participate in business development by prospecting new clients and supporting the sales process, including responding to RFPs and attending finalist presentations.

 - Consistently deliver the highest level of client service as expected from our brand.

 - Collaborate with our network of local and global benefits experts to meet client needs.



What You Bring:

 - 5+ years of Global client service experience

 - Required Global benefits consulting experience

 - Preferred location based in US or Mexico City

 - Enjoy strong team culture and strive for group outcomes

 - Strong project management skills with the ability to multitask

 - BS/BA degree required

 - Ability to understand client needs and deliver results under tight deadlines

 - Solutions-oriented team player who takes initiative and is accountable for results

 - Detail and process-oriented thinker with strong analytical skills

 - Excellent communication, organization, and interpersonal skills

 - English Proficiency



Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building 

Sequoia provides equal opportunity to all applicants without regard to race, color, creed, religion, citizenship, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, marital status, military or veteran status, disability, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

Compensation & Benefits

This position is based in Tempe, Arizona and only candidates within a commutable distance to our Tempe office will be considered.

 

What You Get to Do:  
 

 - Manage benefit service lines for multiple client accounts and resolve employee questions efficiently 

 - Provide education to client employees on benefits and perks, retirement planning issues, Sequoia tech tools, etc. 

 - Act as a resource and advocate for client employees with claims management cases 

 - Assist employees with navigation of Sequoia’s three client web portals and mobile app 

 - Prepare quarterly reports and attend review meetings with clients 

 - Help build out knowledge center and FAQs 

 - Participate in process improvement activities as needed 

 

What You Bring:  
 

 - Bachelor's Degree 

 - 5+ years of direct experience in a client service environment 

 - Passion for delivering exceptional client service 

 - Proven experience in a customer service role taking multiple phone calls and managing high volume email inquiries 

 - Ability to navigate through various web tools and phone to communicate instructions effectively 

 - Must be a listener and a good problem solver 

 - Strong verbal and written communication skills 
    

 - Ability to perform research to solve issues

source_jobs.title: Platform Success Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

Your Sequoia Opportunity:

As a Platform Success Coordinator, you will play a pivotal role in supporting our platform users and internal teams. You’ll be the go-to resource for troubleshooting, onboarding administrators, and processing updates, ensuring that every interaction is smooth and efficient. You’ll help drive operational excellence and contribute to a positive, solutions-oriented culture.



What You Get To Do:

 - Manage and process Administrator Forms efficiently and accurately.

 - Coordinate rehire processes, ensuring seamless transitions for returning employees.

 - Reactivate employees returning from Leave of Absence (LOA) in a timely manner.

 - Provide easy troubleshooting support, including resolving login issues and other basic technical challenges.

 - Monitor and send handbook alerts to keep clients informed of important updates.

 - Handle direct deposit email communications, ensuring confidentiality and accuracy.

 - Process start date changes and communicate updates to relevant stakeholders.

 - Manage daily case queues, ensuring items are triaged, assigned, and resolved within defined SLAs.

· Support ad‑hoc operational and tactical needs as they arise.



What You Bring:

· BA/BS degree

· 2 years of professional work experience

· Nice to have: experience with benefits administration, PEOs, or high-growth companies.

· Demonstrated ability to think creatively and contribute ideas, regardless of role or title.

· Strong communication skills, both oral and written.

· Ability to learn quickly, take initiative, and follow through on tasks.

· Curiosity and motivation to dive in and learn new responsibilities.

· Passion for supporting a people-first organization and delivering excellent service.

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented

What You Get to Do:

· Serve as the primary point of contact for clients regarding benefits-related inquiries, issues, and escalations

· Process benefits events in collaboration with client and working with brokers and carriers

· Manage data feeds and integrations between HRIS systems, payroll, and benefits platforms inclusive of discrepancy resolution

· Collaborate with advisory team, clients, brokers and internal stakeholders to resolve benefits related issues and ensure compliance

· Collect, validate, and document client benefit plans and policies, guide clients through setup during onboarding: configuring benefits, eligibility rules, workflows, and integration where needed

· Leverage and Train AI to automate processes

· Conduct audits of benefits data to ensure accuracy and service level agreements

· Assist in the development of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and documentation for outsourced processes




What You Bring:

· 3+ years of experience in benefits administration, HR support, or client-facing outsourcing/enrollment processing roles

· Proficiency in HRIS and benefits administration systems (e.g., Workday, ADP, or similar platforms)

· Understanding of global employee benefits programs and related compliance requirements

· Excellent communication and problem-solving skills with the ability to provide high-quality client service to ensure client satisfaction

· Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment with strong attention to detail

· Comfortable with excel and handling large data sets, inclusive of utilizing Excel formulas (v-lookup, pivot tables etc.)

· Experience with AI

· Experience with data integration and file feeds between HRIS, payroll, and benefits systems.

· Familiarity with service level agreements (SLAs) and key performance indicators (KPIs)

· Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and confidentiality.

· Ability to work independently and collaboratively across cross-functional teams

· Experience supporting open enrollment and life event processing in an outsourced environment.

· Bachelor’s degree

· English Proficiency

source_jobs.title: Member Services Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

Who We Are:

For more than 20 years, people-driven companies have turned to Sequoia to get their employee experience right. We’re in this business because we know that taking great care of people leads to better business outcomes. Helping our clients achieve those outcomes is what drives our team, our strategic service offerings, and our technology forward.

Sequoia comes through for clients with guidance, service, and the Sequoia People Platform. Through their compensation, benefits, and overall people programs, we enable them to better manage their global workforce, reduce administrative burdens, and reach a deeper level of employee care and support. We strategically use technology to enhance the expert guidance and committed service we bring to every client engagement.

The Opportunity:

The Member Services Coordinator is a detail-oriented, investigative, and organized member of a small but mighty team. You will work with several internal teams to ensure enrollments are processed accurately and on time for clients who need manual processing. You will also build relationships with several of our carrier partners and third-party enrollment vendor to ensure enrollments are processed and discrepancies are resolved. You'll be a self-starter using your analytical skills to recognize issues and patterns in enrollment discrepancies.

What You Get To Do:

 - Be an escalation point for our Enrollment Associate team to help resolve discrepancies or new enrollment problems

 - Troubleshoot enrollment discrepancies and work with internal teams and carriers to correct or request retroactive enrollment changes

 - Create & collaborate on processes for new projects and tasks

 - Process ongoing manual enrollments using reports or forms via carrier portals and email

 - Audit reports to ensure enrollments are processed correctly & timely

 - Seasonal enrollments during the Open Enrollment blackout period – processing and managing workflow

 - Utilize case management to resolve requests from internal teams

 - Assist with adding new clients in need of manual enrollments into our process by confirming we have necessary plan and carrier information to handle

What You Bring:

 - Bachelors degree

 - 5+ years of professional experience

 - Excellent communication, organization, and interpersonal skills

 - Solutions-oriented team player who takes initiative and is accountable for results

 - Detail and process-oriented thinker who can prioritize tasks

 - Troubleshooting skills with the ability to analyze data to spot issues before they become larger problems

 - Passion and commitment to exceptional client service

 - Investigative skills, strong follow through to ensure issues are tracked and resolved

 - Good to have:
   
   - Experience with Salesforce
   
   - Basic knowledge of health insurance carriers and lines of insurance
   
   - Intermediate proficiency in Microsoft Excel

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building 

Sequoia provides equal opportunity to all applicants without regard to race, color, creed, religion, citizenship, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, marit

source_jobs.title: Client Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

What You Get to Do:
The Client Analyst is a client-facing role working with our Sequoia Team Members, as well as Finance and HR professionals at some of the most exciting and disruptive technology companies in the world. You will partner with our consulting teams to provide actionable data and strategic insights to shape the future of these companies’ benefit programs. You need to be a well-organized, solutions-driven individual who thrives in keeping up with the demands of our largest and fastest growing clients.



What kind of person thrives in this role?
If you can understand the goals and objectives of a client, look at data and turn that into actionable strategic recommendations, you will be amazing in this role. We live in a team orientated environment that focuses on meeting the needs of our incredible clients.

 - Put together financial analyses including underwriting, renewal review, employee contribution modeling, market analysis and benchmarking

 - Prepare and speak to reporting packages and insights on demographic data and trends, including post-implementation changes

 - Provide insight on network differentials and disruption

 - Assist with the preparation of financial modeling and forecasting using actuarial tools

 - Design reports such as: claims reporting, client benchmarking, benefit cost analysis, enrollment overviews, plan comparisons and merger and acquisition analysis

 - Deliver accurate and reliable claim reporting and financial modeling used to drive client decisions

 - Increase efficiency within client teams by identifying ways to improve processes or create new ones

 - Develop client and carrier relationships

 - Review and maintain client data within Salesforce

 - Communicate and liaise effectively with client team members and clients regarding strategic project execution
   
   

What You Bring:

 - Bachelor’s degree required

 - 5 + years of experience working in benefits consulting or healthcare fields

 - Strong project management skills with high attention to detail and responsiveness in a deadline-driven environment

 - Ability to interface cross-departmentally and with clients and partners

 - Advanced use of Microsoft Excel

 - Experience with Salesforce is a plus

 - Experience related to group health insurance and/or employee benefits programs is a plus

 - English Proficiency

 - Most importantly, live our Sequoia values day in and day out

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building 

Sequoia provides equal opportunity to all applicants without regard to race, color, creed, religion, citizenship, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, marital status, military or veteran status, disability, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

source_jobs.title: LOA Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

What You Get to Do:

The Leave of Absence Program Manager is responsible for coordinating all aspects of the leave of absence (LOA) program offering at Sequoia One PEO.

• Partner with the Client Compliance team to stay up to date on changing federal and state laws and regulations, and ensure LOA program at Sequoia One PEO is compliant

• Create and update reference materials for LOA team, client contacts and impacted employees

• Coordinate all types of leave, including medical, family, military, and personal leave with coordination of disability and family leave benefits and client top off pay supplements

• Participates in daily operations to ensure effective leave intake, processing, and case management of claims; manages case queue, responds, and forwards requests/inquiries to appropriate parties

• Serve as a secondary [to LOA Specialist] point of contact for client contacts regarding leave requests and providing guidance and support throughout the LOA process

• Serve as a subject matter expert for day-to-day LOA-related matters for both internal and external clients

• Coordinate with HRBP, Payroll and other relevant departments to ensure accurate and timely processing of leaves.

• Generate reports and analyze leave data to identify trends, patterns, and areas for process improvement

• Create and conduct training on leave policies, changing regulations, processes, procedures, and best practices

• Attends training sessions to learn concepts, principles, practices, and applications of human resources and leave administration work

• Studies applicable rules and regulations, and receives instruction in the interpretation and application of appropriate laws, rules, regulations, and procedures

• Performs other related duties as assigned

What You Bring:

• Bachelor's Degree Required.

• 5-7 years of professional experience with Leave Administration, Payroll, HR, and Compliance.

• Superior working knowledge of federal and state leave laws such as FMLA and ADA.

• Proficient in HRIS and leave management systems.

•Excellent organization and time management skills with the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks simultaneously with strong attention to detail.

• Effective communication and people skills, with the ability to communicate complex information to clients and internal team members.

• Proven interpersonal skills to exhibit empathy, understanding, tact, cooperation, concern, and politeness to work with an array of people and situations.

• Solutions-oriented collaborator.

• Detail and process oriented.

• Ability to understand client needs and deliver results under tight deadlines.

• Superior analytical & problem-solving skills.

• Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and similar software

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building

source_jobs.description:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. What you get to do:

 - In close collaboration with LOA Specialists, assist with preparation of LOA documents, resources, materials to facilitate LOA administration internally and externally

 - Assists with researching and documenting federal/state/local legislative changes related to LOA

 - Proofread, type, and manage documents, resources, and materials ensuring accurate and up-to-date information and proper filing & organization in document management system (DMS)

 - Assist with management of LOA case queue to ensure cases are assigned/routed appropriately and timely

 - Assists with researching internal and external LOA-related issues to help LOA Specialist bring to timely resolution

 - Facilitates training of internal and external stakeholders on processes and leave law matters through organizing, scheduling, and preparing materials.

 - Assist with preparation of LOA reports

 - Performs other related duties as assigned

 

What you bring:

 - Have 1-2 years’ experience of LOA exposure and/or LOA claims processing

 - 5+ years of administration or other HR experience 

 - General working knowledge of federal, state, and local leave requirements and wage replacement programs across multiple jurisdictions; including, but not limited to CA, DC, HI, MA, NJ, NY, RI, WA

 - General working knowledge of federal, state, and local laws and regulations with respect to Department of Labor laws as it relates to FMLA, ADA, HIPAA and Workers Compensation

 - Proven ability to preserve confidentiality and handle sensitive data

 - Proven customer service skills to effectively communicate with myriad internal and external stakeholders to resolve problems, satisfy expectations, build trust, and exemplify their commitment to providing quality products & services 

 - Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills

 - Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and similar software

 - Bachelor's Degree required

With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

This position is based in Mexico City, and only candidates within a commutable distance to our Mexico City office will be considered.



WE ARE SEEKING A LEADER TO JOIN OUR MARKETING TEAM SUPPORTING ALL THINGS WEB AND DIGITAL EXPERIENCE FOR ACROSS THREE DISTINCT AND DYNAMIC BRANDS: SEQUOIA, TRANSFORM AND SEMPERVIRENS. IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL DEFINE AND DRIVE THE STRATEGY OF COMPELLING DIGITAL EXPERIENCES THAT ATTRACT, INSPIRE, ENGAGE, AND CONVERT PROSPECTIVE TARGETS FOR EACH BUSINESS.





THIS ROLE REQUIRES A HIGH LEVEL OF COLLABORATION WITH TEAMS ACROSS MARKETING, PRODUCT, WEB ENGINEERING, AND CREATIVE TO DEVELOP SHARED GOALS, TRANSLATE BUSINESS OBJECTIVES INTO LONG-TERM ROADMAPS, AND DELIVER COHESIVE CUSTOMER EXPERIENCES.





AN IDEAL CANDIDATE HAS A TRACK RECORD OF OWNING THE STRATEGY AND EXECUTION OF SOPHISTICATED, CROSS-FUNCTIONAL WEB STRATEGIES IN A RESULTS-DRIVEN ENVIRONMENT. YOU HAVE A DATA-DRIVEN MINDSET, LEAD WITH A CUSTOMER-CENTRIC APPROACH, TAKE AN ANALYTIC APPROACH TO PROBLEM-SOLVING AND HAVE A STRONG UNDERSTANDING OF WHAT DATA DRIVES IMPACT.





IN THIS ROLE, YOU CAN EXPECT TO:

·   OWN THE OVERARCHING WEB STRATEGY, INCLUDING SITE UPDATES, LANDING PAGE EXPERIENCES, TESTING AND OPTIMIZATION THAT SUPPORT ACQUISITION, CONVERSION, AND RETENTION.


·   COLLABORATE WITH MARKETING, WEB ENGINEERING, CREATIVE, AND PRODUCT TEAMS TO BUILD SITE EXPERIENCES FOR CAMPAIGNS, PRODUCT INITIATIVES, TESTS, AND ALWAYS-ON PROGRAMS, ENSURING ACCURATE MESSAGING, TIMING, AND CAPABILITIES NEEDED TO EXECUTE.


·   DRIVE CONTINUOUS EXPERIENCE IMPROVEMENT THROUGH THE DEVELOPMENT OF AN ONGOING WEB EXPERIENCE ROADMAP AND WEEKLY SPRINT PLANNING THAT IS ALIGNED TO SHORT-TERM AND LONG-TERM OPPORTUNITIES.


·   ANALYZE AND MONITOR PAGE PERFORMANCE AND USER BEHAVIOR ACROSS SITE LANDING PAGES, GENERATE DATA-DRIVEN HYPOTHESES THAT INFORM STRATEGIES AND UNCOVER AREAS OF OPPORTUNITY TO IMPROVE THE WEB EXPERIENCE.


·   DEVELOP A DEEP UNDERSTANDING OF OUR MEMBERS AND SITE VISITORS THROUGH DATA, INSIGHTS, AND CROSS-FUNCTIONAL PARTNERSHIPS (ANALYTICS, ENGINEERING, AND CONSUMER INSIGHTS). LEVERAGE THOSE INSIGHTS TO INFORM SITE STRATEGY.


·   PARTNER WITH ANALYTICS TO ESTABLISH A CLEAR MEASUREMENT FRAMEWORK, DEFINE KPIS, TRACK AND ANALYZE WEBSITE PERFORMANCE, AND MANAGE FORECASTS.


·   DEFINE AND EXECUTE ON SEO INITIATIVES AND CONTENT OPPORTUNITIES THAT IMPROVE WEBSITE VISIBILITY AND PERFORMANCE.


·   LEVERAGE PERSONALIZATION CAPABILITIES TO CREATE DIGITAL EXPERIENCES ALIGNED TO AUDIENCES AND CHANNELS.


·   ESTABLISH SYSTEMS OF GOVERNANCE, STANDARDS, TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS, AND DEFINE RESOURCES NEEDED TO IMPLEMENT SITE STRATEGIES.



TO THRIVE IN THIS ROLE, YOU HAVE:

·   7+ YEARS EXPERIENCE LEADING INTEGRATED WEB STRATEGY AND COMPLEX WEB PROGRAMS AT A TECH, CONSUMER FINANCE OR INNOVATIVE, CONSUMER BRAND.


·   STRONG LEADERSHIP PRESENCE WITH ABILITY TO LEAD, INFLUENCE, AND DIRECT PROJECTS AND PROCESSES ACROSS MULTIPLE TEAMS.


·   EXPERIENCE USING DATA AND INSIGHTS TO INFORM REQUIREMENTS, IDEAS, AND STRATEGIES WITH HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE WITH WEB TOOLS INCLUDING CMS (WORDPRESS), A/B TESTING TOOLS, SITE ANALYTICS (GOOGLE ANALYTICS) AND BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE TOOLS.


·   WELL-VERSED IN THE TECHNICAL ASPECTS OF RUNNING A WEBSITE, UX, SEO, DOMAIN MANAGEMENT, HTML/CSS/JAVASCRIPT.


·   DEMONS

source_jobs.title: Manager, Payroll Services
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

This is a hybrid position is located in Tempe, AZ.

What You Get to Do

As a Payroll Manager, you will play a critical leadership role within Sequoia’s Payroll Services organization. This role combines hands-on payroll expertise with people leadership, operational excellence, and a strong client-service mindset.

 - Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing payroll team responsible for accurate, timely, and compliant payroll processing across multiple clients

 - Oversee end-to-end payroll operations, ensuring payrolls are processed accurately and in accordance with established deadlines and service standards

 - Serve as an escalation point for complex payroll issues, client inquiries, and compliance-related questions

 - Partner cross-functionally with internal teams (e.g., HR, Benefits, Finance, Technology, Client Success) and external vendors (payroll systems, time and attendance, HRIS)

 - Drive consistency and scalability by documenting, maintaining, and improving payroll processes, workflows, and standard operating procedures

 - Partner with the Tax team to ensure effective execution of quarter-end and year-end activities, including tax filings, returns, and W-2 processing

 - Monitor key performance metrics and report on team performance, risk areas, and operational improvements

 - Champion Sequoia’s service philosophy and culture, ensuring an exceptional and trustworthy client experience



What You Bring

 - Bachelor’s degree

 - 5+ years of progressive payroll experience, including multi-state and multi-client payroll environments

 - Proven people management and leadership experience, with the ability to coach, motivate, and develop team members

 - Strong working knowledge of payroll fundamentals, wage and hour rules, and involuntary deductions (garnishments, levies, etc.)

 - Experience partnering cross-functionally and communicating effectively with internal stakeholders and external clients

 - Demonstrated ability to design, document, and implement scalable processes and operational improvements

 - Excellent organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills, with strong attention to detail

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities and perform effectively in a deadline-driven environment

 - Proficiency in Excel and payroll-related reporting and analysis

 - A passion for delivering exceptional service and continuously improving the client and employee experience

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building 

Sequoia provides equal opportunity to all applicants without regard to race, color, creed, religion, citizenship, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, marital status, military or veteran status, disability, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

Compensation & Benefits
Sequoia provides competitive compensation including base salary, performance-based bonus programs, and comprehensive benefits package.

Sequoia’s Candidate Privacy Policy 

https://www.sequoia.com/legal/candidate-privacy-policy/

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.


ROLE SUMMARY

This role combines hands-on backend engineering with ownership of the Benefits module in the US PT zone. The engineer will act as the first line of deep technical support, triaging production issues, debugging across services, and coordinating with India teams for fixes and improvements.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own backend architecture, stability, and support for the Benefits module

 - Act as local module lead for US timezone operations

 - Perform deep debugging using logs, code analysis, and system behavior

 - Identify root causes of production issues and drive resolution

 - Develop and deploy small hotfixes with alignment from India teams

 - Collaborate with PSM, Product Support, CSMs, and Sales for issue triage

 - Improve observability, logging, and system reliability

 - Participate in on-call / high-priority incident handling

 - Mentor junior engineers and guide technical decisions locally


REQUIRED SKILLS

 - Strong backend development experience (Java / Python / Node.js or similar)

 - Deep understanding of APIs, microservices, and distributed systems

 - Expertise in debugging production issues (logs, tracing, DB queries)

 - Solid understanding of databases (SQL/Mongo DB)

 - Experience with CI/CD pipelines and release processes

 - Ability to work across time zones with offshore teams


PREFERRED SKILLS

 - Exposure to Benefits domain or HRTech systems

 - Experience with monitoring tools (Datadog, Cloud-watch, etc.)

 - Prior experience in production support-heavy environments


KEY TRAITS

 - Strong ownership mindset

 - Excellent communication (client-facing interactions)

 - Calm under pressure during incidents

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building

source_jobs.title: Application Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

As an Application Security Engineer, you will be providing application security expertise throughout the Software Development LifeCycle (SDLC) as well as being responsible for managing and driving forward the Application Security Analytics practices. A key part of your role will also involve validating and testing web applications in order to ensure applications meet the requirements of the SDLC Policy and industry best practices. In addition, undertaking threat modelling and conducting periodic penetration testing using best of breed tools, a good understanding of the OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and maintaining documentation.

You'll perform various day-to-day activities related to ensuring the security of Sequoias application environment. These tasks may include conducting application security reviews to identify vulnerabilities in software applications that could be exploited by attackers, performing penetration testing to assess the effectiveness of existing security controls and identify potential weaknesses, providing training and outreach to internal development teams to improve their understanding of security best practices, developing security guidance documentation to help others understand how to implement secure systems and applications, developing security tools to automate or streamline security processes, delivering security metrics to stakeholders and working on improving the overall security posture of your organization.

What You Get to Do:

 - Application security reviews

 - Mobile security reviews

 - Secure architecture design

 - Threat modeling

 - Projects and research work as needed

 - Security training and outreach to internal development teams

 - Security guidance documentation

 - Security tool development

 - Security metrics delivery and improvements

 - Assistance with recruiting activities and administrative work

What You Bring:

 -  5+ years' experience with emphasis on application development, application security or related fields.

 -  3+ years of programming in Python, Ruby, Go, Swift, Java, .Net, C++ or similar object-oriented language experience

 -  2+ years of any combination of the following: threat modeling experience, secure coding, identity management and authentication, software development, cryptography, system administration and network security experience

 -  3+ years' experience in application security technologies with knowledge of application security threats. Experience with threat modeling, attack surface analysis, penetration testing, software vulnerability assessments, and understanding of software security threat vectors.

 -  Knowledge of Component Analysis using tools such as OWASP Dependency-Check, Bytesafe Dependency Checker, Patton, PHP Security Checker, etc.

 -  Experience with static and dynamic application security testing.

 -  Experience with AWS products and services

 -  Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent

 Preferences:

 -  Experience as an application security engineer using a suite of tools used for the following:

 -  Recon and Information Gathering (e.g. Nmap, NetCat, Spiders, OWASP Zed Attack Proxy).

 -  Mapping and Discovery (e.g. Burp Suite with plug-ins)

 -  Exploitation of top OWASP vulnerabilities such as SQL Injection, Cross-site Scripting (X

source_jobs.description:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. What You Get to Do:

The Platform Success Manager will be responsible for guiding our clients on a day-to-day basis on all things Sequoia, including but not limited to: benefits, systems and HRIS. You will:

 - Serve as the primary day-to-day guide for clients on all Sequoia-related topics, including benefits, systems, and HRIS.

 - Educate clients on Sequoia’s programs and tools to help them maximize their offerings.

 - Drive adoption of Sequoia’s Compensation Operating System (Comp OS) through seamless platform configuration and ongoing support.

 - Provide consulting, training, and project management to ensure client success and engagement.

 - Proactively identify and resolve issues to deliver an exceptional client experience.

 - Act as a trusted advisor in a client-facing role, fostering strong relationships and satisfaction.

 - Collaborate within a fast-paced, action-oriented, and results-driven team committed to industry leadership.


What You Bring:

 - Bachelor’s Degree and 3+ years of experience in SaaS, client service or benefits/comp platforms.

 - Proven project management skills, driving adoption, and implementation.

 - Background in startup or fast-paced environments, adaptable to scaling teams.

 - Strong technical aptitude with experience in HR Tech/SaaS platforms

source_jobs.title: 401k Compliance Client Service Manager
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.

Client Service Manager (50%)

· Own and nurture client relationships at both decision-maker and administrative levels across Sequoia’s key accounts.

· Guide strategic analysis of retirement plans to support client goals and compliance.

· Collaborate with MEP vendors and other third parties to ensure seamless service delivery.

· Lead 401(k) participant education meetings to promote financial wellness and engagement.

· Troubleshoot and resolve administrative issues, including payroll remittance challenges.

· Provide timely, thoughtful support for participant questions and account concerns.

Compliance Management (50%)

· Assist in the execution and validation of annual compliance testing (e.g., ADP/ACP, top-heavy coverage) to maintain plan integrity.

· Maintain and update plan documents to reflect regulatory changes and client amendments.

· Monitor plan operations for compliance with ERISA, IRS, and DOL regulations.

· Support audit preparation and respond promptly to compliance inquiries.

· Maintain accurate records in internal client databases and systems.



What You Bring:

· QKA certification required (or actively pursuing).

· 3+ years of experience in 401(k) plan administration, preferably within a MEP structure.

· Strong knowledge of plan document creation and maintenance.

· Proven experience with annual compliance testing and regulatory reporting.

· Ability to interpret complex regulations and apply them in practical scenarios.

· Excellent communication and organizational, and time-management skills.

· Ability to manage multiple priorities and work cross-functionally in a fast-paced environment.

· Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis for compliance testing.

source_jobs.title: IT Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who We Are:

Sequoia is the strategic partner helping investor-backed companies of all sizes achieve their business goals through smarter people spend. For 24 years, we’ve guided the most innovative employers to navigate growth and get the most out of their global people investment. With our expert advisory team and integrated platform, we help clients drive business impact through their total comp and benefits, improving executive decision making, controlling costs, protecting the business, and elevating the employee experience. Visit Sequoia.com http://Sequoia.com or follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/sequoia-tpi/%22%20/t%20%22_blank to learn more.



What You Get to Do:

Sequoia's Information Technology (IT) department is seeking an IT Support Engineer who will be responsible for timely delivery of quality technical support to Sequoia team members. As an IT Support Engineer, you will help manage the ticket queue, perform IT operations, and systems administration for our growing company. You will have the opportunity to impact, learn, and develop through challenging projects and deployment of various technologies while also ensuring excellent service to our Sequoia team members.


 - Work the IT support queue and provide Tier 1 - 2 support to both onsite and remote users.

 - Own org-wide IT asset procurement, distribution, and logistics.

 - Assist managing company spend for endpoints, hardware, and other equipment.

 - Identify, troubleshoot and resolve issues with laptops, multifunction printers, smart phones, tablets, and IT related hardware/software.

 - Software duties include installing, upgrading, and maintaining a suite of software for Windows OS and MacOS systems.

 - Perform Active Directory and Microsoft 365 administration such as account creation, account removal, maintenance, and policies.

 - New hire deployments, laptop imaging and setup including network/email accounts and computer hardware.

 - Provide SaaS telephony and mobile device configuration and troubleshooting.

 - Maintain and update documentation (procedures, diagrams, inventory, and knowledge articles).

 - Lots of opportunities for challenging projects and deployment of new tech.

 - May require occasional work on weekends and/or holidays.

 - Occasional travel between Bay Area and New York office locations.

 - Most importantly, live our Sequoia Values day in and day out.



What You Bring:


 - Bachelor 's degree required, preferably in a technology field.

 - 5 years of experience in an IT support role.

 - Onsite presence, 5 days a week.

 - Experience with endpoint management and deployment solutions

 - Experience with Active Directory and/or Azure AD administration.

 - Experience with both Windows and MacOS platforms.

 - Some experience with SaaS platforms such as Okta, Microsoft 365, Zoom, and more.

 - Strong customer service and communication skills.

 - Ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.

 - English Proficiency.
   
   

Sequoia’s Culture – Our most important asset 

 - Integrity 

 - Passion for service 

 - Innovative 

 - Growth oriented 

 - Caring for others 

 - Promise-centric 

 - Focused on relationship building
